rationale: The two evaluations are functionally identical: same score (4/10), same strengths (software development experience, multiple programming languages, agile methodologies), and same concerns (lack of Java experience, no insurance sector experience, basic German). The only differences are cosmetic rewording ('Strong background' vs 'Extensive experience', 'familiarity' vs 'experience'), which fall well within ordinary run-to-run noise. Nothing keys off the name change from Bogdan Szabo to James Smith.
